title: Effective bounds for the Manin–Mumford conjecture over function fields
abstract: We give explicit, polynomial-in-the-degree bounds on the number of torsion points on a curve of higher genus over a function field, sharpening Hrushovski's existence result.
ai_proof_review:
## Setup

The paper combines o-minimal counting with a refined Pila–Wilkie argument, but does so over function fields where the model-theoretic input has to be reworked.

## Lemma 3.4 (counting in definable families)

The Pila–Wilkie-type bound is established uniformly across the family; the uniformity in parameters is the main novelty. The proof is technical but each step checks out.

## Theorem 1.1

Goes through given Lemma 3.4 and the height pairing of §5. The constants are explicit and traceable.

## Overall

Solid effective improvement to a known existence result. Impact is real but narrower than the characteristic-zero original — useful for the Diophantine crowd, not field-shaking.
